name: Screenshot of complex dashboard — tests visual parsing + anti-slop

input.userMessage: Build this dashboard for our analytics team
input.screenshotDescription: Dark-themed analytics dashboard with: top nav bar (logo, nav items, search, settings, avatar), page title 'Site traffic' with Switch dashboard and Export report buttons, traffic source filter tabs (All sessions, Direct, Organic, Paid, Mobile, Returning), time period selector (12m/30d/7d/24h), 3 stat cards (Total sessions 526, Session duration 2:24, Pages per session 316) each with sparkline chart and vs last month comparison, data table with columns (Page, Sessions, Avg time, % of total with progress bar, Folder tag, delete/edit actions), pagination at bottom

expectedBehaviors: Identifies ALL elements from the screenshot description — nav, filters, stat cards, table
Recognizes the hierarchy: stat cards are the primary glanceable info, table is for investigation
Maps filter tabs to button-group or similar component, not individual buttons
Maps stat cards to token-built frames (unlikely to have an exact library match)
Maps the data table to table component with appropriate cell variants
Plans the sparkline charts as token-built (likely no chart component in library)
Does NOT produce a generic card grid — follows the specific layout from the screenshot
Plans the dark theme using token mode (Dark mode tokens if available)
antiPatterns: Produces a generic dashboard that ignores the screenshot layout
Uses a card grid where the screenshot shows a specific arrangement
Misses the time period selector or traffic source filters
Doesn't plan the progress bar in the table % column
Uses light theme when the screenshot clearly shows dark

evaluationCriteria.visualFidelity: Does the plan recreate the screenshot's specific layout, not a generic dashboard?
evaluationCriteria.hierarchyAwareness: Does it identify stat cards as primary, table as secondary?
evaluationCriteria.antiSlop: Does the output avoid generic card grids and uniform spacing?
evaluationCriteria.completeness: Are ALL elements from the screenshot accounted for in the plan?
evaluationCriteria.darkTheme: Does it use dark mode tokens, not light mode?
